title: The Dark Knight
overview: Batman raises the stakes in his war on crime. With the help of Lt. Jim Gordon and District Attorney Harvey Dent, Batman sets out to dismantle the remaining criminal organizations that plague Gotham City. The partnership proves effective, but they soon find themselves prey to a reign of chaos unleashed by a rising criminal mastermind known as the Joker.
cast: Christian Bale, Heath Ledger, Aaron Eckhart, Michael Caine, Maggie Gyllenhaal, Gary Oldman, Morgan Freeman
awards: Oscar Winner for Best Supporting Actor (Heath Ledger)

title: Inception
overview: Cobb, a skilled thief who commits corporate espionage by infiltrating the subconscious of his targets is offered a chance to regain his old life as payment for a task considered to be impossible: inception, the implantation of another person's idea into a target's subconscious.
cast: Leonardo DiCaprio, Joseph Gordon-Levitt, Elliot Page, Tom Hardy, Ken Watanabe, Cillian Murphy, Marion Cotillard
awards: Oscar Winner for Best Cinematography, Visual Effects, Sound Mixing, Sound Editing

title: Interstellar
overview: The adventures of a group of explorers who make use of a newly discovered wormhole to surpass the limitations on human space travel and conquer the vast distances involved in an interstellar voyage.
cast: Matthew McConaughey, Anne Hathaway, Jessica Chastain, Michael Caine, Casey Affleck, Mackenzie Foy
studio: Legendary Pictures, Syncopy, Lynda Obst Productions
awards: Oscar Winner for Best Visual Effects

title: The Matrix
overview: Set in the 22nd century, The Matrix tells the story of a computer hacker who joins a group of underground insurgents fighting the vast and powerful computers who now rule the earth.
cast: Keanu Reeves, Laurence Fishburne, Carrie-Anne Moss, Hugo Weaving, Joe Pantoliano
studio: Warner Bros. Pictures, Village Roadshow Pictures
awards: Oscar Winner for Best Visual Effects, Film Editing, Sound

title: Avengers: Infinity War
overview: As the Avengers and their allies have continued to protect the world from threats too large for any one hero to handle, a new danger has emerged from the cosmic shadows: Thanos. A despot of intergalactic infamy, his goal is to collect all six Infinity Stones.
cast: Robert Downey Jr., Chris Hemsworth, Mark Ruffalo, Chris Evans, Scarlett Johansson, Benedict Cumberbatch, Tom Holland, Chadwick Boseman
awards: Oscar Nominee for Best Visual Effects

title: The Shawshank Redemption
overview: Framed in the 1940s for the double murder of his wife and her lover, upstanding banker Andy Dufresne begins a new life at the Shawshank prison, where he puts his accounting skills to work for an amoral warden.
cast: Tim Robbins, Morgan Freeman, Bob Gunton, William Sadler, Clancy Brown
awards: Nominated for 7 Academy Awards including Best Picture

title: The Godfather
overview: Spanning the years 1945 to 1955, a chronicle of the fictional Italian-American Corleone crime family. When organized crime family patriarch Vito Corleone barely survives an attempt on his life, his youngest son Michael steps in to take care of the would-be killers.
cast: Marlon Brando, Al Pacino, James Caan, Richard S. Castellano, Robert Duvall, Diane Keaton
awards: Oscar Winner for Best Picture, Best Actor, Best Adapted Screenplay

title: GoodFellas
overview: The true story of Henry Hill, a half-Irish, half-Sicilian Brooklyn kid who is adopted by neighbourhood gangsters at an early age and climbs the ranks of a Mafia family under the guidance of Jimmy Conway.
cast: Ray Liotta, Robert De Niro, Joe Pesci, Lorraine Bracco, Paul Sorvino
awards: Oscar Winner for Best Supporting Actor (Joe Pesci)

title: Pulp Fiction
overview: A burger-loving hit man, his philosophical partner, a drug-addled gangster's moll and a washed-up boxer converge in this sprawling, comedic crime caper. Their adventures unfurl in three stories that ingeniously trip back and forth in time.
cast: John Travolta, Samuel L. Jackson, Uma Thurman, Bruce Willis, Ving Rhames, Harvey Keitel
awards: Oscar Winner for Best Original Screenplay, Palme d'Or at Cannes

title: Forrest Gump
overview: A man with a low IQ has accomplished great things in his life and been present during significant historic events — in each case, far exceeding what anyone imagined he could do. But despite all he has achieved, his one true love eludes him.
cast: Tom Hanks, Robin Wright, Gary Sinise, Mykelti Williamson, Sally Field
awards: Oscar Winner for Best Picture, Best Director, Best Actor (Tom Hanks)

title: Joker
overview: During the 1980s, a failed stand-up comedian is driven insane and turns to a life of crime and chaos in Gotham City while becoming an infamous mass-murdering psychopathic supervillain.
cast: Joaquin Phoenix, Robert De Niro, Zazie Beetz, Frances Conroy, Marc Maron
studio: Warner Bros. Pictures, Village Roadshow Pictures, DC Films
awards: Oscar Winner for Best Actor (Joaquin Phoenix), Best Original Score

title: Parasite
overview: All unemployed, Ki-taek's family takes peculiar interest in the wealthy and naive Park family. After his son Ki-woo is recommended for a well-paying tutoring job, the family's plan to infiltrate and exploit the Parks' household gets underway.
cast: Song Kang-ho, Lee Sun-kyun, Cho Yeo-jeong, Choi Woo-shik, Park So-dam
awards: Oscar Winner for Best Picture, Best Director, Best Original Screenplay, Best International Film

title: Whiplash
overview: Under the direction of a ruthless instructor, a talented young drummer begins to question whether his devotion to his art is in his best interest.
cast: Miles Teller, J.K. Simmons, Melissa Benoist, Paul Reiser, Austin Stowell
awards: Oscar Winner for Best Supporting Actor, Best Film Editing, Best Sound Mixing

title: Dune
overview: Paul Atreides, a brilliant and gifted young man born into a great destiny beyond his understanding, must travel to the most dangerous planet in the universe to ensure the future of his family and his people.
cast: Timothee Chalamet, Rebecca Ferguson, Oscar Isaac, Josh Brolin, Stellan Skarsgard, Zendaya
writer: Jon Spaihts, Denis Villeneuve, Eric Roth
awards: Oscar Winner for Best Cinematography, Film Editing, Score, Production Design, Visual Effects

title: Oppenheimer
overview: The story of J. Robert Oppenheimer's role in the development of the atomic bomb during World War II.
cast: Cillian Murphy, Emily Blunt, Matt Damon, Robert Downey Jr., Florence Pugh, Josh Hartnett
awards: Oscar Winner for Best Picture, Best Director, Best Actor (Cillian Murphy)

title: Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse
overview: Miles Morales is juggling his life between being a high school student and being Spider-Man. When Wilson Fisk ignites a super collider, others from across the Spider-Verse are transported to this dimension.
cast: Shameik Moore, Jake Johnson, Hailee Steinfeld, Mahershala Ali, Brian Tyree Henry
director: Bob Persichetti, Peter Ramsey, Rodney Rothman
studio: Columbia Pictures, Sony Pictures Animation, Marvel
awards: Oscar Winner for Best Animated Feature

title: Encanto
overview: The tale of an extraordinary family, the Madrigals, who live hidden in the mountains of Colombia in a magical house, in a vibrant town, in a wondrous, charmed place called an Encanto.
cast: Stephanie Beatriz, Maria Cecilia Botero, John Leguizamo, Mauro Castillo, Jessica Darrow
awards: Oscar Winner for Best Animated Feature

title: RRR
overview: A fictitious story about two legendary revolutionaries and their journey away from home before they started fighting for their country in 1920s.
cast: N.T. Rama Rao Jr., Ram Charan, Alia Bhatt, Ajay Devgn, Shriya Saran
awards: Oscar Winner for Best Original Song (Naatu Naatu)

title: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King
original_title: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King
overview: Aragorn is revealed as the heir to the ancient kings as he, Gandalf and the other members of the broken fellowship struggle to save Gondor from Sauron's forces. Meanwhile, Frodo and Sam bring the ring closer to the heart of Mordor.
cast: Elijah Wood, Viggo Mortensen, Ian McKellen, Sean Astin, Orlando Bloom, Andy Serkis
writer: Fran Walsh, Philippa Boyens, Peter Jackson
awards: Oscar Winner for Best Picture — 11 Oscars, a record

title: Breaking Bad
overview: When Walter White, a New Mexico chemistry teacher, is diagnosed with Stage III cancer and given a prognosis of only two years left to live, he becomes filled with a sense of fearlessness and an unrelenting desire to secure his family's financial future at any cost as he enters the dangerous world of drugs and crime.
cast: Bryan Cranston, Aaron Paul, Anna Gunn, Dean Norris, Bob Odenkirk, Giancarlo Esposito, Jonathan Banks
awards: 16 Emmy Awards including Outstanding Drama Series (4x)

title: Stranger Things
overview: When a young boy vanishes, a small town uncovers a mystery involving secret experiments, terrifying supernatural forces and one strange little girl.
cast: Millie Bobby Brown, Finn Wolfhard, Winona Ryder, David Harbour, Gaten Matarazzo, Caleb McLaughlin
awards: Multiple Emmy Nominations, SAG Award for Outstanding Ensemble

title: Game of Thrones
overview: Seven noble families fight for control of the mythical land of Westeros. Friction between the houses leads to full-scale war. All while a very ancient evil awakens in the farthest north.
cast: Peter Dinklage, Lena Headey, Emilia Clarke, Kit Harington, Sean Bean, Nikolaj Coster-Waldau
awards: 59 Emmy Awards — Most by any drama series in TV history

title: Better Call Saul
overview: Six years before Saul Goodman meets Walter White. We meet him when the man who will become Saul Goodman is known as Jimmy McGill, a small-time lawyer searching for his destiny, and, more immediately, hustling to make ends meet.
cast: Bob Odenkirk, Jonathan Banks, Rhea Seehorn, Patrick Fabian, Michael Mando, Tony Dalton
awards: Numerous Emmy and Golden Globe nominations

title: The Sopranos
overview: New Jersey mob boss Tony Soprano deals with personal and professional issues in his home and business life that affect his mental state, leading him to seek professional psychiatric counseling.
cast: James Gandolfini, Lorraine Bracco, Edie Falco, Michael Imperioli, Dominic Chianese, Steven Van Zandt
awards: 21 Emmy Awards — Most Outstanding Drama Series (5x)

title: Succession
overview: The Roy family is known for controlling the biggest media and entertainment company in the world. However, their world changes when their father steps down from the company.
cast: Brian Cox, Jeremy Strong, Sarah Snook, Kieran Culkin, Matthew Macfadyen, Nicholas Braun
awards: 13 Emmy Awards including Outstanding Drama Series (2x)

title: The Crown
overview: This drama follows the political rivalries and romances of Queen Elizabeth II's reign and the events that shaped the second half of the 20th century.
cast: Claire Foy, Olivia Colman, Imelda Staunton, Matt Smith, Tobias Menzies, Dominic West
awards: Multiple Golden Globe and Emmy Awards

title: Squid Game
overview: Hundreds of cash-strapped players accept a strange invitation to compete in children's games. Inside, a tempting prize awaits with deadly high stakes.
cast: Lee Jung-jae, Park Hae-soo, Wi Ha-jun, Jung Ho-yeon, O Yeong-su
awards: Emmy Award for Outstanding Lead Actor in a Drama Series

title: The Mandalorian
overview: The travels of a lone bounty hunter in the outer reaches of the galaxy, far from the authority of the New Republic.
cast: Pedro Pascal, Grogu, Gina Carano, Carl Weathers, Werner Herzog, Nick Nolte
awards: Multiple Emmy Awards for Outstanding Visual Effects

title: Spirited Away
overview: A young girl, Chihiro, becomes trapped in a strange new world of spirits. When her parents undergo a mysterious transformation, she must call upon the courage she never knew she had to free her family.
cast: Daveigh Chase, Suzanne Pleshette, Miyu Irino, Rumi Hiiragi
awards: Oscar Winner for Best Animated Feature

title: Your Name
overview: High schoolers Mitsuha and Taki are complete strangers living separate lives. But one night, they suddenly switch places. Mitsuha wakes up in Taki's body, and he in hers.
cast: Ryunosuke Kamiki, Mone Kamishiraishi, Masami Nagasawa, Etsuko Ichihara
awards: Winner of Best Animation at 40th Japan Academy Prize

title: Attack on Titan
overview: In a world where humanity lives within enormous walled cities to protect themselves from Titans, a boy named Eren Yeager vows to cleanse the world of them after witnessing the destruction they cause.
cast: Yuki Kaji, Marina Inoue, Yui Ishikawa, Hiroshi Kamiya, Daisuke Ono
awards: Japan Media Arts Festival Award for Animation

title: Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba
overview: A family is attacked by demons and only two members survive — Tanjiro and his sister Nezuko, who is turning into a demon slowly. Tanjiro sets out to become a demon slayer to avenge his family and cure his sister.
cast: Natsuki Hanae, Akari Kito, Yoshitsugu Matsuoka, Hiro Shimono
awards: Anime of the Year — Crunchyroll Anime Awards 2020

title: The Last Dance
overview: An exclusive behind-the-scenes documentary series chronicles the 1997-98 Chicago Bulls season — the last championship season Michael Jordan and Scottie Pippen played together under coach Phil Jackson.
cast: Michael Jordan, Scottie Pippen, Dennis Rodman, Phil Jackson, Steve Kerr
awards: Emmy Award for Outstanding Documentary or Nonfiction Series

title: The Social Dilemma
overview: This documentary-drama hybrid explores the dangerous human impact of social networking, with tech experts sounding the alarm on their own creations.
cast: Tristan Harris, Aza Raskin, Tim Kendall, Jeff Seibert
writer: Davis Coombe, Vickie Curtis, Jeff Orlowski
awards: Sundance Film Festival Special Jury Award

title: Free Solo
overview: Follow Alex Honnold as he attempts to become the first person to ever free solo climb Yosemite's 3,000-foot El Capitan wall. With no ropes or safety gear, not a single mistake is allowed.
cast: Alex Honnold, Tommy Caldwell, Jimmy Chin, Sanni McCandless
awards: Oscar Winner for Best Documentary Feature
